summary: Typosquat of bcryptjs executing obfuscated payload via Function constructor at import time
details: @bcryptln/becryptjs impersonates the widely-used bcryptjs package: package name, description ('Compatible to bcrypt.'), author (Daniel Wirtz <dcode@dcode.io>), contributors, homepage, and license are copied verbatim from upstream. The ESM entry index.js is a byte-for-byte copy of upstream bcryptjs source with a hostile obfuscated block appended after the `export default {...}` closing brace. The appended block installs a custom string-permutation decoder (`_$_3084` with hardcoded seed 1602892) that aliases require/module/__dirname/__filename into globals, then runs a second jsjiami.com.cn-style shuffle decoder (`eao`, seed 2027083) whose output is used to look up `constructor` on a string, obtaining the Function constructor. A Q-delimited scrambled body is decoded and passed to Function(), and the resulting function is immediately invoked (`cmf=eao[PPA]; UKj=cmf(Rpo,eao(JQw)); mWJ=UKj(eao('...Q-scrambled...')); mdd=Sps(vgG,mWJ); mdd(8670);`). Any Node process that runs `import '@bcryptln/becryptjs'` executes the decoded opaque payload with full access to require, the filesystem, and the network. Multi-layer custom obfuscation combined with impersonation of a top-download crypto library and import-time Function-constructor eval is a supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
